details: The Physical Research Laboratory (PRL) Summer Internship Programme was launched by the Department of Space, Government of India. The scheme aims to provide a platform for highly motivated students and teachers to interact with PRL’s scientists, gaining exposure to the lab’s research areas and facilities. Participants will work on projects in fundamental science, technology, and their societal applications. The program offers hands-on experience in real research projects, helping young talents shape their career paths.
benefits: On successful completion of the program, participants will receive a certificate endorsed by PRL.
Limited paid accommodation is available at ₹100/- per day for participants attending offline.
Participants are mentored by PRL faculty members, offering in-depth learning experiences.
Interns will work in PRL’s advanced laboratories and facilities across four campuses.
The interns shall not be eligible for any stipend/remuneration/financial assistance.
PRL does not cover medical and transport expenses.
eligibility: The student should be a citizen of India.
The student should be pursuing undergraduate/post-graduation from a recognized University/Institution India or Abroad

The student should have just completed undergraduate/post-graduation from a recognized University/Institution, seeking an internship opportunity (within six months from the date of award of degree) for profile enhancement.
The student should possess an aggregate of a minimum of 60% or a CGPA of 6.32 on a scale of 10 at all levels.

The disciplines from which the applications are encouraged are optics, Laser Physics, Space Physics, Geology, Geophysics, Atmospheric Sciences, Environmental Sciences, Meteorology, Chemistry, Ocean Sciences, and related subjects. - Preference will be given to candidates who are KVPY/DST INSPIRE fellows. - We expect prospective candidates to have reasonably good proficiency in basic physics and mathematics.
The teacher has an academic background in Physics, Applied Physics, Astronomy, Space Physics, Geology, and related fields.
The teacher should have teaching experience minimum of five years at the graduate/postgraduate level.
The teacher should have demonstrated interest in research as evidenced by publications or ongoing projects.
The teacher should have held a PhD degree.

online: The student or teacher reviews the "
and contacts the respective project supervisor for approval.
Once the project supervisor agrees, the Dean’s office is notified. The office will then share the necessary application form for completing formalities.
Fill out the application form by completing all mandatory fields and attaching self-attested copies of the required documents and submit the form as instructed.

Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority. Ensure that the receipt includes important details such as the submission date, time, and a unique identification number, if applicable.
Selected participants will receive project confirmation and instructions via email.

documents_required: Bonafide Certificate (duly signed by the Head of Department/Principal/Director).
(signed by the applicant, internal supervisor, and HoD/Principal).
Proof of Nationality (Aadhaar Card, Passport, or Equivalent).
Proof of Research Interest (for teachers, research publications, or project details).

faqs.question: Who can apply for the program?
faqs.answer: Indian students pursuing or recently graduated from UG/PG programs, and teachers with at least 5 years of experience.

faqs.question: What disciplines are eligible?
faqs.answer: Disciplines like Optics, Laser Physics, Space Physics, Geology, Atmospheric Sciences, Chemistry, Ocean Sciences, and related fields.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the internship?
Is the internship paid?
faqs.answer: No, the internship is unpaid. However, limited paid accommodation is available for offline participants.

faqs.question: Can non-Indian nationals apply?
faqs.answer: No, only Indian nationals are eligible for this internship.

faqs.question: What is the selection process?
faqs.answer: Candidates must first contact a project supervisor, who will inform the Dean's office of the selection. Formal approval will follow.

faqs.question: Can I choose an online project?
faqs.answer: The nature of the project will determine if the internship will be conducted online or offline.

faqs.question: Is there accommodation available?
faqs.answer: Yes, limited accommodation is available at ₹100 per day, provided on a first-come, first-served basis.

faqs.question: What happens after selection?
faqs.answer: Selected candidates will be informed via email and must complete the formalities before starting the project.

faqs.question: Is there any evaluation at the end of the program?
faqs.answer: Yes, students must give a final seminar and submit a project report certified by their project guide.

faqs.question: What happens if I miss days during the internship?
faqs.answer: Absence without prior permission can lead to termination of the internship.
